what_it_does: Renders a design-system preview card that maps platform entities to geometric brand shapes with labels, subtitles, and inline SVG illustrations.
observations: Includes @dsCard metadata for a Brand card named "Entity shape system" with a 700x280 viewport.
Loads shared card styling from _card.css and adds file-local CSS for layout, typography, and shape presentation.
Presents five entity examples in a five-column grid: User Portal, Property Wizard, Virtual Hubs, Vi, and Other.
Uses inline SVG for each shape: circle, hexagon, octagon, diamond with line-fill pattern, and rounded rectangle.
Uses gold stroke/fill accents, CSS variables for typography and foreground colors, and one inline style for the Vi label.
standouts: The file defines a direct visual association between each named entity and one shape.
The lead copy states the shapes should be used consistently across decks, diagrams, and product UI.
The Vi diamond uses both geometry and a line-fill pattern to distinguish it from the other shapes.
conventions: Uses a self-contained HTML preview card with metadata, shared CSS import, and local styles.
Uses CSS variables for fonts and text colors while hardcoding SVG accent colors.
Keeps shape labels as absolutely centered HTML text over SVGs instead of embedding all labels inside SVG text.
Uses short label and subtitle pairs under each visual example.
concerns: The five-column grid and fixed 110px shape wrappers may be narrow for smaller viewports.
The accent color is hardcoded repeatedly as #E0C010 instead of using a CSS variable consistently.
The Vi label uses inline styling for font size, letter spacing, and nested color.
The SVG pattern id "diapatt" is generic within the document.
notes: A compact brand reference card that demonstrates entity-to-shape assignments using inline SVG and centered labels.
questions: Why is the User Portal represented with a dollar sign inside the circle?
Should the 700x280 card adapt for narrower preview surfaces?
Is the rounded rectangle intended only as a generic fallback or as a reusable entity category?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it shows the entity shape system and labels how each shape should be associated with an entity.
intention_signals: It seems intended to give designers and product builders a quick visual reference for applying consistent entity shapes across branded materials and interfaces.
novelty: Combines simple geometric entity markers with semantic labels and a patterned diamond treatment for Vi.
contribution: Provides a neutral ledger entry for entity-specific geometric markers, including the fallback rounded rectangle and the visual treatment details needed to preserve those associations in a fused system.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML design-system preview card showing five platform entities mapped to specific geometric brand shapes with inline SVGs, labels, and subtitles.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for documenting and previewing a consistent entity shape system for use across decks, diagrams, and product UI.
